name: MIB-626 raised circulating NAD metabolites in older overweight adults
summary: MIB-626 adds a pharmaceutical-grade NMN target-engagement signal, but the trial was short and did not test durable function or clinical outcomes.
statement: In a 32-participant randomized 14-day study of overweight or obese adults aged 55 to 80 years, 1000 mg MIB-626 once or twice daily was well tolerated and produced dose-related increases in blood NMN, NAD, and NAD-metabolome measures versus placebo.
population_or_model: Overweight or obese adults aged 55 to 80 years
quantitative_note: Blood NMN AUClast rose 1.7-fold with 1000 mg once daily and 3.7-fold with 1000 mg twice daily; blood NAD and downstream metabolites increased in a dose-related pattern.
caveats: Circulating NAD movement is target engagement, not proof of tissue rejuvenation.
The trial was short, small, and biomarker-focused.
Functional, disease, durability, and mortality endpoints were not tested.
